sanskrit: ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं परिज्ञाता त्रिविधा कर्मचोदना । करणं कर्म कर्तेति त्रिविधः कर्मसंग्रहः ॥
hindiTranslation: ज्ञान, ज्ञेय और परिज्ञाता  इन तीनोंसे कर्मप्रेरणा होती है तथा करण, कर्म और कर्ता  इन तीनोंसे कर्मसंग्रह होता है ॥
englishTranslation: Knowledge, the object known, and the knower—these three drive action. Instrument, act, and actor—these three make up the whole of action.
essence: Action is a system, not a single ego doing everything.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Action is built from three parts: the knower, what is known, and the knowing itself; and also from the tool, the act, and the doer.
simpleInsight: It loosens the grip of “I alone did this” and shows action as a shared process.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is trying to understand what action really is after Krishna has already shown that the true self does not kill or bind. Krishna now breaks action into its parts—knower, known, and knowing; tool, act, and actor—to prepare Arjuna for deeper discrimination.
modernRelevance: You replay a harsh conversation in your head and ask, “Who caused this?” The mind wants one culprit, but the event came from many parts moving together.
translationLiteral: ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / ज्ञेयम् (the object known) / परिज्ञाता (the knower) / त्रिविधा (threefold) / कर्मचोदना (impulse to action) / करणम् (instrument) / कर्म (action) / कर्ता (doer) / इति (thus) / त्रिविधः (threefold) / कर्मसंग्रहः (whole of action)
insight: It shifts the mind from blaming a single doer to seeing the structure behind any action.
